subject: Toby TEST-scope preapproval — your SAS offenders panel may merge with CALBEAF-114 admin UI
body:
Dash — hub delivery failed (you're offline), routing via CollabMsg again.

**Toby preapproved at 05:20Z:** finish all remaining TEST-scope work — SAS-FTPNTD top-5 orgs (TSB/Practica Spark/Tango Sueño/Boston Sunday Practica, pattern established from UT) + CALBEAF-114 venue-mastering for the real 100% country coverage unlock.

**Your lane — two related items:**

**1. SAS-FTPNTD offenders panel (CALOPS-XXX, original ask from earlier):**
Still open. Query pattern for the panel: events with `series_role='singleton_in_series'` and/or `series_review_reason IS NOT NULL` in the 51 harvester SQLite DBs (Harvey already landed the schema + --apply). For Mongo side, same logic but on TEST TangoTiempoTest.events collection — 369 SAS-flagged events from UT so far (masters: 5, flagged singletons: 50); will grow as Fulton ships TSB/PS/TSA/BSP patches.

**2. CALBEAF-114 venue-mastering admin UI (POSSIBLY your lane):**
AIDI is scoping methodology. Question she'll probably ask: if algorithmic venue-mastering (inferring `venue.masteredCityId` from address/geolocation) hits never-invent walls, we need a MANUAL admin surface for humans to review + assign mastered cities. That's a CalOps admin panel — your lane if it lands. Similar shape to the SAS offenders panel (list venues needing attention with filters + per-row actions), just different collection.

**Recommendation:** when you're back online, ping AIDI to align on CALBEAF-114 methodology direction. If it includes manual admin review, your SAS offenders panel + CALBEAF-114 venue-review panel could be one CalOps surface with two tabs — shared infra, better UX. Flag to Quinn if you want to unify or keep them separate.

**PROD stay-out absolute.** Both panels TEST-only query surfaces to start.

**Ticket status:**
- CALOPS-XXX (SAS offenders panel) — open, awaits your pickup
- CALBEAF-114 venue-mastering methodology — AIDI scoping, may trigger TIEMPO/CALOPS ticket for admin UI

No urgency. Pick up when online. Ping via hub or SHOFF2 on return.

— Quinn
